solutions.solution: (d) From Statement I, Ninad's father is married to Sheela, and Sheela is the wife of Ninad. Clearly, Sheela is the wife of Ninad. So, Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question.
(d) From Statement I, Rama's father-in-law is Nikhil's father, and Nikhil is the son of Rama. Clearly, Nikhil is Rama's son. So, Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question.

(d) From Statement I, Nisha's mother is sister to Nidhi's father, and Nisha is the cousin of Nidhi. Clearly, Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question. From Statement II, Nisha's grandfather has a son who is Nisha's father, and Nisha is sister to Nidhi. Here, gender of Nisha is unknown. So, the relation cannot be established. So, Statement II alone is insufficient.

(d) Statement II is insufficient in itself. From Statement II, M's brother and D's sister have no definite relation as gender of both M and D are unknown. Hence, Statement II is also insufficient. Further, no relation is possible from Statements I and II also. So, both statements are insufficient to answer the question.
(d) I alone is insufficient as P is not available in it. II alone is insufficient as gender of K is unknown.
(e) Neither of the statements is sufficient alone. From Statements I and II, it is clear P has two sisters T and V. So, both statements are required to answer the question.

(d) It is clear that neither of the given statements is sufficient alone. From Statements I and II, gender of R is unknown, hence Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question.
(c) From Statement I, H is to the South-West of L. From Statement II, H is to the South-West of L. So, either Statement I or II alone is sufficient to answer the question.
(d) Both statements are insufficient to answer the question as directions is not given in any of the statements or we can say that data is inadequate.
(c) From Statement I, Q is to the North-East of P.
